headline: Check suspicious email headers before you act
subtext: Hush Line includes an email validation tool so recipients can inspect raw headers, review authentication checks, and save a report.
image_alt_text: Social graphic built from a mobile Hush Line screenshot showing the Email Validation tool. The screen shows tabs for Email Validation and Vision Assistant, a box of raw email headers, a purple Validate Headers button, a Download Report button, and the start of a results summary below.

social.bluesky:
When an email looks questionable, recipients need a quick way to inspect the headers before they rely on it.

Hush Line's Email Validation tool checks DKIM, SPF, and DMARC from raw headers and lets you download a report. Learn more at https://hushline.app.
social.linkedin:
When a source email looks off, the first question is simple: did it really come from where it says it did?

Hush Line includes an Email Validation tool for recipients and staff. Paste raw headers, inspect DKIM, SPF, and DMARC results, and download a report you can keep with your review notes.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
social.mastodon:
When an email looks questionable, recipients need a fast way to check the headers before they rely on it.

Hush Line's Email Validation tool lets you paste raw headers, review DKIM, SPF, and DMARC results, and download a report for your records.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

rationale: The latest screenshot release is fresh for production use, but recent archive history is dominated by directory posts, including a newsroom directory post on 2026-03-30. This mobile light screenshot avoids another directory repeat, stays within the target dark-mode share, and highlights a concrete recipient workflow that matters to journalists, legal teams, and other trusted recipients reviewing suspicious email evidence. It is the strongest non-directory candidate with recent shipped work metadata in the shortlist.
copy_brief: Write for recipients and staff using Hush Line day to day, not for platform admins.

matched_pull_requests.title: Show default message status text in settings

title: Tools - Email Validation status: appears valid (artvandelay)
